nodes.name: Twilio WhatsApp Webhook
nodes.notes:
Receives incoming WhatsApp messages from Twilio.
Twilio sends POST with form-urlencoded body containing:
- Body: message text
- From: sender number (whatsapp:+91...)
- To: your Twilio WhatsApp number

Configure this webhook URL in your Twilio WhatsApp Sandbox:
https://<your-n8n-domain>/webhook/twilio-whatsapp-webhook

nodes.name: Extract Booking URL
nodes.notes:
Extracts the Kochi Metro booking URL from the WhatsApp message.
Pattern: https://prutech.org/KMRL/#/manage/ticket/*
If no URL found, sets hasValidUrl=false for error handling downstream.

nodes.name: Has Valid URL?
nodes.notes:
Checks if a valid Kochi Metro booking URL was found.
True → Continue to journey extraction.
False → Send error reply via WhatsApp.

nodes.name: Parse Journey Details
nodes.notes:
Parses origin, destination, and passenger count from the message text.
Supports multiple formats:
- 'from X to Y'
- 'X -> Y' / 'X → Y'
- 'X to Y'
- Line-by-line (origin, destination, passengers)
- 'N passengers' / 'for N people' / 'one ticket'

Defaults: Aluva → Palarivattom, 1 passenger.
Includes known Kochi Metro station names for fuzzy matching.

nodes.name: Book Kochi Metro
nodes.notes:
Calls the Kochi Metro booking API.
POST https://mindmetroapi.midhunapi.me/book
Body: { url, origin, destination, passengers }

Retries once on failure (network issues).
Timeout: 30 seconds.
Errors route to the error output for graceful handling.

nodes.name: Booking Successful?
nodes.notes:
Checks if the booking API returned success: true.
True → Extract payment URL and send success reply.
False → Extract error message and send failure reply.

nodes.name: Build Success Reply
nodes.notes:
Builds the WhatsApp success reply message with the payment URL.
Includes route details and passenger count for confirmation.

nodes.name: Build Failure Reply
nodes.notes:
Builds the WhatsApp failure reply message.
Includes the error message from the API response.

nodes.name: Send Success WhatsApp
nodes.notes:
Sends the booking success + payment URL back to the user via WhatsApp.
Uses the original sender's number as the recipient.
'From' is your Twilio WhatsApp number (received as 'To' in the incoming webhook).

nodes.name: Send Failure WhatsApp
nodes.notes: Sends the booking failure error message back to the user via WhatsApp.

nodes.name: Build No URL Reply
nodes.notes: Builds a helpful error message when no valid booking URL was found in the incoming message.

nodes.name: Send No URL WhatsApp
nodes.notes: Sends the 'no valid URL found' error reply back to the user via WhatsApp.

nodes.name: Build HTTP Error Reply
nodes.notes:
Handles cases where the HTTP request to the booking API itself failed
(e.g., timeout, network error, API unreachable).
Builds a user-friendly error message.

nodes.name: Send HTTP Error WhatsApp
nodes.notes: Sends the HTTP error message back to the user via WhatsApp.
